title: Malak (Rodian)

{{Quote|I wouldn't be in business if there wasn't a market for what I sell. Just you remember that!|translated=1|Malak|<ref name="g11" />}}
'''Malak''' was a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Slavery/Legends|enslaver]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]] and active during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. She was known to attack ill-defended communities with her [[Malak's gang|gang]] and kidnap victims, sometimes severely injuring them. Although Malak abducted the [[Abyssin/Legends|Abyssin]] [[Myo/Legends|Myo]] from his native [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss (Abyssin)/Legends|Byss]] to enslave him, he later escaped from her. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Malak was active and at large, but she was also tracked by the authorities of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

==Biography==
Malak was a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]].<ref name="g11" /> She worked as a [[Slavery/Legends|enslaver]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], both leading up to<ref name="air">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> and after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], which saw the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] suffer a major defeat. She was the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of her own [[Malak's gang|gang of raiders]], leading them in incursions against unsuspecting, unprotected villages. During these raids Malak used a modified [[snare gun]] to capture living prey.<ref name="g11" />

By around [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Malak was still a petty criminal. She attacked<ref name="air" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Byss (Abyssin)/Legends|Byss]], which was inhabited by [[Abyssin/Legends|Abyssins]], who were a common target for slavers by that point.<ref name="gg4">''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]''</ref> She captured at least one Abyssin, [[Myo/Legends|Myo]], and took him aboard her [[Malak's starship|starship]]. She then left Byss and made a stop at [[Kinun Depot]] to [[Fuel/Legends|refuel]] her [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. Myo took the opportunity to escape from Malak.<ref name="air" />

The Empire listed her as the subject of one of their [[TracSheet]]s, which were used to record charges against criminals. Malak was filed under number 11706900-AGTY-233476. She was accused with several charges, including the activities of a slaver and the possession of illegal weapons. She was also suspected of [[Pirate/Legends|piracy]]. Although the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], which was established shortly after the Battle of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], had executed several campaigns to end slavery from 4 ABY onward, Malak was still at large and active as of [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].<ref name="g11" />

==Personality and traits==
Malak was easily recognized because she had lost some of the spikes Rodians had on their heads, had several scars on her neck and face—including on the snout. She had pale [[Color/Legends|green]] skin and was 1.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height. Malak was an accomplished [[Torture/Legends|torturer]] without any mercy and an expert in her specialized business activities. She loved violence and relished making raids on defenseless and unsuspecting communities. She was also a shrewd negotiator, a good [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and [[Navigator/Legends|navigator]], and a capable fighter. Her leadership skills were limited to her gang. Malak was a violent [[Speciesism|speciesist]] who despised [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] from [[Species/Legends|species]] other than her own, and cared only about [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]], disregarding the state, health, or lives of the people she sold. Sometimes she accepted private contracts for "specialty goods." Malak justified her activities by saying that there was a niche market for them.<ref name="g11" />

==Equipment==
Malak often wore a number of valuable [[Ring/Legends|rings]] on her [[Limb/Legends|hands]]. She also commonly carried a snare gun and [[Magnacuffs/Legends|magnacuffs]] to use on her targets, along with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]].<ref name="g11" />

==Behind the scenes==
Malak was first mentioned in [[Rick D. Stuart]]'s ''[[Galaxy Guide 11: Criminal Organizations]]'' ([[1994]]). This book, a supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', includes a portrait of Malak drawn by [[Terry Pavlet]] and a quote by the character. The quote is described as a translation without specifying the original [[Language/Legends|language]] in which Malak spoke. The character was also mentioned in the Myo character entry in the book ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' ([[1995]]), a resource supplement to ''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game''.
